evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ensure data quality through testing, documentation, and observability
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities and requirements repeatedly emphasize testing, documentation, observability, and standards over speed.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Gaming/entertainment platform analytics with no financial reporting or regulatory language present, so errors affect business decisions without regulatory consequence.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Establish dbt standards including naming, layering, and documentation
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The role is asked to establish standards and lead greenfield development, suggesting the analytics function is still growing rather than fully mature.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, build, and maintain analytics-ready data models using dbt
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (dbt, Airflow, Dagster, Prefect) and precise technical practices (dimensional modelling, macros, CI/CD) make the day-to-day work clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Act as strategic partner helping teams understand data possibilities
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The emphasis is on serving data consumers and building the modelling layer itself, placing this in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Lead greenfield analytics development from raw data through business-facing models
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: The role leads and establishes standards (strategic) but also collaborates to translate stakeholder requirements (execution), giving a mixed profile.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Ensure data quality through testing, documentation, and observability
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and data quality are framed as an owned responsibility with an action verb, not just a listed skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: reliability and quality concerns recur but are framed as delivery/craft quality, not compliance or stakeholder trust risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Lead greenfield analytics development from raw data through business-facing models
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Explicit 'greenfield' language dominates the infrastructure-building responsibilities.
